why does the eyewall replacement cycle happens?

Essentially, the previous eyewall of a storm gets too small for the storms size. It collapses on itself as a secondary, larger, eye wall that forms around it. As the new eye forms, it's larger and can support even higher winds than the previous after clearing out.
